hypothesis: If a single classifier-free-guided score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state molecular dynamics data and used with score-based interpolation (isodensity or averaging) followed by Score-Aligned Ascent driven by the conditional score difference as an approximate reaction coordinate, then ASTRA will generate accurate transition-state geometries with committor distributions centered near 0.5 and higher TS identification success rates than linear/geodesic interpolation or ablated SBI/SAA variants across 2D potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin, and the tested chemical reaction because the conditional scores encode inter-state directionality that guides reverse-SDE sampling into TS regions and SAA refines samples along the true reaction coordinate.
